_purpose: Orchestrator continuity across Claude Code sessions. The HUMAN talks to ONE AI (the orchestrator); that AI dispatches scope-specific work via the Agent tool. When the human starts a fresh Claude Code session, the new orchestrator MUST read this file early in startup so it knows: what was last being worked on, what's pending, what decisions were just made, what scopes are currently claimed, what to NOT re-do.
_protocol: Update this file at every significant decision/checkpoint. Treat it like a running session journal — newest at the bottom of recent_decisions, oldest pruned. The aggregator script `node scripts/agent-status.js` complements this with per-scope status from data/coordination/sessions/*.json.
_what_vs_why: As of v0.283.x: this file records WHY decisions were made (running journal). `docs/PRIORITIES.md` records WHAT comes next (strict-ranked backlog). Both must be updated when scope changes. Don't put 'next action' here — put it in PRIORITIES.md.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.703.0 NATIVE ANSWERS 1:1 VOICE CALLS (the ring-forever bug, worst cross-client defect in the parity audit). Design: reuse the ENTIRE proven voice-room audio path (str0m browser-compatible WebRTC: SDP byte-identical to RTCSessionDescription, DTLS-SRTP, Opus/RTP; cpal capture; the lib.rs mic pump + VoiceConnected/VoiceFrame events) by introducing a reserved pseudo-room CALL_ROOM_ID=__call__: emit_voice_signal branches on it to wear the web 1:1 webrtc_signal envelope (bare offer/answer/ice, OBJECT data, no room_id) instead of voice_room_signal. Inbound: lib.rs routes bare offer/answer/ice into submit_voice_signal(__call__) GATED on the accepted call peer (never auto-answer unsolicited media offers); dc_* keep their DataChannel path. Control plane: voice_call ring/accept/reject/hangup handled (was an explicit discard); busy auto-reject (in call, ringing, or in a voice room); relay stamps from_name. UI: Accept/Decline modal + in-call bar (connecting/connected + Hang up) drawn on the Chat page AND as a global overlay from lib.rs so a ring is answerable from any page (web caller gives up in 30 s). NEW WebrtcManager Command::ClosePeer + close_peer(): hangup drops the str0m Rtc immediately, else its is_alive guard would refuse the SAME peer's next call until ICE timeout. Session lifecycle: want_session now voice_active_room OR call_active; Closed event also clears call_active (covers web tab-close with no hangup message). Scope: ANSWER only; native-initiated calls are the next increment.
recent_decisions.why: Operator picked chat parity and approved proceeding with my suggestion (this bug ranked worst: silent cross-client failure). The str0m room path already interops with browsers, so the honest increment was signaling + control + UI, not a new audio engine. recent_decisions.decision: v0.721.0 FOLLOW-DIRECTION BADGES (operator bug report: cannot see one-way follow states). ROOT CAUSE: the relay follow_list has ALWAYS sent following + followers, but native consumed only following and dropped followers; native also ignored the follow_update broadcast entirely — so native never knew who follows you (web had the full feature all along: updateFriendIndicators + myFollowers, and its .peer/.peer-name selectors still match the rebuilt rail, so web was never broken). FIX: GuiState.chat_followers + chat_following_keys (raw key sets — chat_friends filters against ONLINE users and drops offline people); follow_list stores both; new follow_update handler keeps them live; members rows paint a follow-direction arrow (both-ways=friends/success, right=you follow, left=follows-you/warning) with hover explanation; profile modal shows the relationship line + "Follow back" button label; follow/unfollow update local sets immediately. GLYPH LESSON: U+2190 left-arrow TEXT glyph is TOFU in the app font (snapshot-proof) even though CLAUDE.md lists the Arrows block as reliable — added U+2190/U+2194 to icon_glyph_lint BROKEN_GLYPHS, painted the arrows as shapes instead (new icons::paint_arrow_left/paint_arrow_both), fixed the cosmos Reverse tooltip bare arrow char.

recent_decisions.decision: TEXTURE-BUG INVESTIGATION: scout finding ADJUDICATED AND REFUTED — do NOT apply its proposed shader fix. The scout claimed all 11 noise shaders have an axis-collapse bug in mix(mix(a,b,u.x), mix(c,d,u.x), u.y) and proposed changing the second inner mix to u.y. VERIFIED WRONG by reading the shaders directly: that pattern IS canonical bilinear value noise (corners (0,0),(1,0) blend the BOTTOM edge along X with u.x; (0,1),(1,1) blend the TOP edge along X with u.x; the outer mix blends the two edges along Y with u.y) — procedural_material.wgsl:121-130 and pbr_simple.wgsl:151-162 are textbook-correct, and hash2 is the standard Dave Hoskins hash12. Applying the scout fix would BREAK every procedural surface. The scout even contradicted itself (its buggy-vs-correct example bodies are identical). REMAINING HYPOTHESES for the real colored-lines bug, in order: (1) f32 PRECISION COLLAPSE at world-space UV magnitudes — floors deliberately use uv=[x0,z0] world coordinates (src/ship/fibonacci.rs:525, intentional anti-smearing); fract(p*k) at large |p| quantizes unevenly per axis producing exactly axis-aligned lines — check the homestead world-origin offset + the noise frequency constants callers multiply UVs by; (2) a specific caller passing a near-constant coordinate on one axis (check the wall/ceiling UV builders, not just floors); (3) driver-specific fract() behavior. NEXT SESSION: reproduce in-game with the F-key screenshot tool at known coordinates near/far from origin — if lines worsen with distance from origin, hypothesis 1 is confirmed and the fix is rebasing UVs to a local anchor (e.g. room-local coordinates) before scaling.
recent_decisions.why: Field-session directive #3 asked to investigate the texture bug. The investigation produced a negative-result deliverable that PREVENTS a regression: the plausible-but-wrong scout report would have shipped broken noise across 11 shaders if applied unverified. Verification-of-subagent-work norm earned its keep.
recent_decisions.verification: Direct read of procedural_material.wgsl + pbr_simple.wgsl noise functions against the canonical bilinear form; scout report cross-checked and self-contradiction identified. No code changed.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.733.0 FUEL LOOP SLICE 2 + STORE ACTION. (1) MachinePower::Generator { watts, fuel_lph (serde default 0) } — wind parses unchanged as a free always-on source; the backup genset becomes Generator(2000 W, 1.5 L/h) + its steel drum. (3) STORE: the card panel lists per-item deposit buttons for class-accepted pack items (no-mixing respected; wrong-class deposits impossible from the UI) and shows for EMPTY vessels — caught during release-notes writing that without Store the fuel chain dead-ended at the pack (the drum starts empty), so it shipped in the same release rather than as a "known follow-up".

recent_decisions.decision: v0.742.0 MOVE-MY-FILES (in-app storage-mode migration). storage.rs gains copy_tree / copy_identity / unique_backup_name / migrate_to_portable / migrate_to_per_user under a strict contract: copy-first, commit-last (marker written/removed only after all copies; failed marker-removal rolls back the data rename so the install stays fully portable), delete-never (originals kept; exe-side data renamed data-backup-N so detect() lands on Installed), identity NEVER overwritten (existing destination config.json renamed to config.json-backup-N first). Settings>Data gets the switch button with 2-click confirm + ONE-migration-per-session lock (button becomes a restart note after success). Legacy->portable skips the per-user data dir so stale data never clobbers live modded files.
recent_decisions.why: Second v0.707 follow-up, GUI-first (nobody should hand-copy an identity file). ADJUDICATION NOTE: the pre-ship adversarial review claimed 2 CRITICAL data-loss defects (double-migration identity clobber; half-state after rollback); both REFUTED on inspection (the copy source is always the LIVE copy per mode(), so overwrites are always freshest-over-stale, and the rollback end-state is a fully consistent portable install with inert per-user copies). The identity-backup + per-session-lock hardenings were applied anyway because they kill the whole failure class for a few lines of code - cheaper than being right about the reasoning forever.
recent_decisions.verification: 3 new unit tests (copy_tree nesting/counts, copy_identity backs-up-before-overwrite, backup-name collision); 781 lib tests; native+relay checks; emdash+theme+glyph lints; independent review agent on the diff.

recent_decisions.decision: FABLE HANDOFF STRATEGY: spend the final Fable day on DECIDED DESIGN, not code. Rationale: successor models execute well against clear specs; open-ended design synthesis over 19 systems + the vision is where the stronger model adds unique value. Deliverables: the three bible docs (v0.744.2) + PRIORITIES handoff block. The closure ladder deliberately orders stakes-before-content, sinks-before-faucets, one-player-economy-before-community, reuse-before-new-systems; every rung activates something that already exists rather than inventing systems.

The survey proved the instinct right: the game has MORE built than anyone documented (complete dormant combat pipeline, authored-but-unloaded economy/creature/ability data, a live seventh governance domain) - so the design work was 90 percent connecting, 10 percent inventing.

recent_decisions.decision: RUNG 6 SHIPPED v0.749 (environment matters), taken before networked rung 5. Calls: harvest items via an EXPLICIT plants.csv column (script-generated, validated against items.csv, prefix search as fallback) rather than growing the prefix list; outdoor = grow-area id ends _field (indoor immunity is the point: the tradeoff justifying indoor farming becomes mechanical); climate slows growth (25% off-season floor, 5%/degree outside the temp window, 20% floor) but never kills (death stays water/RF-driven); station gate strips the _0 item suffix to the machine type, fails OPEN when no home layout exists, and creative bypasses. validate-data caught two real authoring bugs pre-ship: a water_purifier catalog key collision (my kitchen duplicate would have silently overwritten the water-loop purifier def) and 4 unwired powered stations (buildability test) - both fixed, stations joined the battery bus.

recent_decisions.decision: RUNG 8 FIRST HALF SHIPPED v0.750 (gear is real). Calls: equipment.csv references the EXISTING items.csv clothing family (no new items needed - jackets/coats/gloves/boots/hats/backpacks were already there, inert); the persisted Outfit component is THE single worn state for gear AND cosmetics (a cosmetic = gear with no stats row; last-writer-wins per slot); equip refuses rather than losing anything (swap-back must fit); EquipmentRegistry mirrors the status-effect stat grammar with net_stat_multiplier + stat_add_total so gear and buffs can never diverge. Consumed stats v1: speed, carry_capacity, cold/heat_resist. Armor columns are schema-ready but inert until CombatSystem registers (deliberate).
recent_decisions.why: Progression doc rungs 1-3 needed no new systems - pure connection of existing components (Outfit, net_stat_multiplier pattern, FoodSystem temp drain). The survey line "insulation, oxygen gear, speed, carry-capacity are one hookup away" proved exactly right.

recent_decisions.decision: RUNG 7 SHIPPED v0.751 (passive livestock). Calls: renewable_product is a CREATURES.CSV column (item:amount:regrow_seconds) because what an animal yields belongs to the species, while data/entities/livestock.ron holds only homestead PLACEMENT (species, count, anchor machine instance, spread, placeholder tint) - adding a species is a CSV row, placing it is a RON row. The long-unconsumed Harvestable component is the regrow state; collect() is a pure function shared by the E-press bridge and tests. Animals spawn READY so a fresh homestead demonstrates the loop immediately. Wander is a deterministic per-animal lissajous around a per-placement anchor (no physics, no pathfinding) - cheap, testable, alive-looking. Pack-full collect REFUSES and restores the yield to the animal (vendor_buy rule: paid/earned goods are never lost). Wild spawning, hostility, taming, and loot_table drops all deferred to the combat arc - they read this same registry.
recent_decisions.why: creatures.csv had 92 fully-authored species and zero loaders; Harvestable had zero consumers. Rung 7 passive-first closes the smallest full loop (see animal -> collect product -> use in kitchen/loom) without pulling in combat, and gives the homestead its first living inhabitants.

recent_decisions.decision: RUNG 8 ABILITIES INCREMENT SHIPPED v0.753. Calls: KEPT the richer authored schema (mana/stamina/damage/healing/duration columns) instead of the progression doc's proposed effects-verb rewrite - the loader derives behavior from existing columns, zero row rewrites, and the combat arc reads the same registry. flavor column added via type mapping (spell|psionic->fantasy, tech->tech, ability->real). Castable = skill gate met, NO separate grant table or KnownAbilities component (learn-by-doing: your level IS your grant); level-1 gates are baseline-open matching the recipe gate-at-2+ convention, which makes the ungated first_aid/campfire and level-1 cauterize/heal/holy_light/repair castable by a fresh player. Both cost columns pay from the energy vital until a stamina vital exists (casting makes you tired - abilities join the survival economy). Offensive rows load but refuse honestly until combat provides targets. Cooldowns are session-scoped in the system (like machine timers), published to the GUI via an ability_cooldowns channel.
recent_decisions.why: spells.csv was the largest fully-authored zero-loader file left (110 rows). The self-scoped healing slice ships real value now (the first player-initiated healing beyond regeneration), and the registry + request channel are exactly the pipeline the combat arc needs.

recent_decisions.decision: COMBAT ARC INCREMENT 1 SHIPPED v0.760. Calls: ONE damage pipeline (abilities queue DamageEvents on a channel; CombatSystem owns mitigation/death/loot) rather than abilities dealing damage directly; loot delivers to the killer pack via a loot_drops channel (ground-item entities are a later arc, and v1 the player is the only attacker); corpse decay ONLY despawns Creature entities (a Dead player belongs to the respawn flow); ALL 128 missing loot items generated with keyword-derived categories/physicals rather than hand-adding the 5 the starter herd needs - the zero-drop guarantee now covers every creature tier before it becomes fightable; fantasy damage strings map onto the 5 combat categories (fire/ice thermal, lightning/psychic/holy/shadow energy, poison chemical, physical/true kinetic) instead of growing the enum; armor-on-equip deliberately deferred until hostiles can hit back (untestable-in-game before that).
recent_decisions.why: The closure ladder completed; PRIORITIES named the combat arc the default next big arc (most already-authored content unlocked: 110 offensive ability rows, 92 creature loot tables). This increment makes the fight-kill-loot loop real against the existing passive herd.
recent_decisions.verification: channel_hit_kills_and_drops_loot + armor_mitigates_and_corpses_decay + offensive_cast_queues_damage_at_a_target + every_loot_drop_resolves_to_a_real_item; 813 lib tests; both feature checks; all four lints; validate-data.

recent_decisions.decision: COMBAT ARC INCREMENT 2 SHIPPED v0.761 (hostiles fight back). Calls: ENHANCED the existing AISystem (snapshot gains is_player/max_health/name; predators include players as prey; the attacking state queues bites on damage_events with a 1.5s cooldown; the system integrates its own movement since nothing else moves AI entities) instead of a parallel hostile pass - FEATURES rule 14. Bite damage derives from data (health_base * 0.15 capped 25) rather than a new CSV column. Wild spawns are their own data file (wild_spawns.ron, absolute positions away from home) separate from homestead livestock placement. Loot delivery gated on source_is_player (a wolf kill is not your loot). Armor rebuilds from the worn Outfit EVERY frame (trivial cost, covers saved-outfit load + instant equip changes) instead of only on equip events. Passive livestock keep the amble; AI creatures skip it.
recent_decisions.why: Combat had one direction. Hostiles-that-bite make armor real (deferred from v0.760 as untestable), give death-by-combat a name, and complete the fight-or-be-fought loop with all-authored data.
recent_decisions.verification: predator_hunts_the_player_and_bites + player_combat_death_records_cause_and_wolf_kills_grant_no_loot + armor_from_worn_sums_and_caps; 816 lib tests; both feature checks; all four lints (wiring rebuilt post AISystem de-listing); validate-data.

recent_decisions.decision: PIVOT TO VALIDATION + CO-PRESENCE, and shipped unified-chat increment 1a (v0.771: in-game #general feed). Candid call made to the operator: a long build streak produced depth nobody had witnessed; the corridor bugs he found on first real play prove tests cannot catch what needs eyes. Recommended shifting from net-new systems to (a) proving two real humans can share the world and (b) a human-in-the-loop field-report cadence. Operator agreed and directed unified chat + VPS join. Increment 1a is deliberately READ-ONLY (paint-only HUD feed, zero input-focus risk) because the fiddly part is typing in the cursor-locked 3D world - stage that next once the feed is confirmed reading right. Key discovery: the relay auto-connect already runs globally every frame (gated on seed_unlocked + onboarding_complete), so co-presence chat was already connected, just not shown.
recent_decisions.why: The mission is uniting humans; the near-term success bar is playable multiplayer on VPS. Everything built (mall, trade, chat, ship) only matters with a second person present. A shopping mall with one occupant is a lonely place. Highest-leverage next work is proving the together-ness, not a sixteenth system.
recent_decisions.verification: 870 lib tests; both feature checks; emdash/theme/glyph lints. The feed itself is the operator field test (read-only, low risk) - relaunch v0.771 build, look bottom-left in the world.

recent_decisions.decision: Shipped unified-chat increment 1b (v0.772): the in-world chat is now INTERACTIVE. Enter opens a compact bottom-left panel (chat_input_active) that frees the cursor and disables look + WASD move so you can read, click, and type in the same relay chat as the Chat page/website; channel switcher + focused input send via the existing send_composed_content path; Esc/Close returns to gameplay. Also fixed the field-report bugs: the v0.771 HUD feed was hardcoded to channel=="general" (switching to #announcements never updated the header or loaded its messages) - the feed now follows chat_active_channel and is suppressed while the interactive panel is open. Staged deliberately: 1a read-only feed first (confirmed reading right by the operator), THEN 1b the fiddly cursor-locked typing, per the validation-in-the-loop cadence.
recent_decisions.why: The near-term success bar is playable multiplayer on VPS and the mission is uniting humans; typing to a shared channel from inside the 3D world (not just a separate Chat page) is the first tangible together-ness. Staging read-only before interactive kept the risky input-focus + cursor-lock work out of a blind increment - the operator field-tested the feed before the typing landed.
recent_decisions.verification: 870 lib tests; cargo check native + relay; emdash/theme/glyph lints. Interactive behavior (Enter opens, cursor frees, look disabled, type/send, channel switch updates header + loads messages) is the operator field test on the v0.772 build.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.773: fixed the v0.772 in-world chat panel into a real typing mode from the operator field report. Three bugs, one root cause - while the panel was open the game still processed gameplay input. (1) The letter/number hotkeys (inventory i, 1-9 ability hotbar, F/R/H/B) gated only on active_page==None, still true with the panel open, so typing i opened the inventory; added a single guard right after the modifier tracking in the KeyboardInput arm that short-circuits ALL gameplay key handling while chat_input_active (egui already received the keystroke via on_window_event, so the char still types). (2) Esc opened the pause menu instead of closing the panel; the guard handles Esc (close + return) and returns before the global menu-Esc handler. (3) Click-away now dismisses the panel but KEEPS the typed text (chat_input untouched, only send clears it) so the big box never lingers over the combat view; detected via egui pointer any_click outside the Area rect; a MouseInput guard swallows that same dismissing click so it never also swings a tool / places a machine. Plus: opening mid-run clears held movement on BOTH movers (new CameraController::stop_movement + zeroing the InputState the ECS PlayerController reads) because the guard swallows the key-release events.
recent_decisions.why: The v0.772 panel worked (channel switch + send confirmed by the operator) but was unusable for actual typing - every letter double-fired a hotkey and Esc bounced you to the menu. This is exactly the validation-in-the-loop cadence paying off: shipped read-only (1a), then interactive (1b), operator field-tested and found the input-plumbing gaps, fixed same-day. The all-chat/DMs/groups view modes (1c) still wait behind a panel that now types cleanly.
recent_decisions.verification: 870 lib tests; cargo check native + relay; emdash/theme/glyph lints. Interactive behavior is the operator field test on the v0.773 build: type without triggering hotkeys, Esc closes to gameplay, click the world to dismiss (text preserved), no drift after opening while walking.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.774: investigated shared-world co-presence to scope the mission-critical join flow, and found it is ALREADY built end-to-end (client auto-joins over the chat socket + streams position; relay validates + broadcasts welcome/joined/position/left; net_sync renders remote avatars). The real gap was that it was INVISIBLE, so shipped legibility: a HUD top-left presence indicator (Shared world + server host + live roster of who else is present), a GuiState roster mirrored from the ECS each frame, and a relay-side name stamp so a late joiners world snapshot carries real player names (not Player) both directions. Chose this over the 1c chat view modes because co-presence is the named mission-critical proof and you cannot validate what you cannot see - this turns the two-client test into something the operator can watch work.
recent_decisions.why: The near-term success bar is playable multiplayer on VPS and the mission is uniting humans. Co-presence is the single highest-value proof; the discovery that the mechanism already works (just invisibly) means the highest-leverage move was to make it visible + testable, not to build a sixteenth system. The two-client live test is the remaining step and needs a second client/person, so making presence legible is the necessary precursor.
recent_decisions.verification: 870 lib tests; cargo check native + relay; emdash/theme/glyph lints. CI Deploy-to-VPS triggered (relay changed - name stamp needs the live relay rebuild; roster works regardless). The operator two-client test is the real validation: two native instances, distinct identities, both in-world + connected -> roster ticks up + avatars appear.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.775: fixed the character-select launcher so the server you are connected to is actually visible + joinable. Field report: the launcher Servers section said "No servers yet. Add one from the Chat sidebar" even while connected to united-humanity.us for chat, because it only listed explicitly-SAVED chat_servers, never the live connection. Fix (showroom.rs): the launcher now shows the live connection as a virtual first Servers entry (server_display_name(server_url), marked connected, sentinel id __connected__, deduped against saved servers by url); draw_server_details special-cases the sentinel to read server_url and shows live /api/server-info; and the stale disabled "Connect - arrives with multiplayer" button is replaced by a working "Enter World" for the connected server (drops into the shared world; co-presence auto-joins over the live connection, roster in the top-left). Saved-but-not-connected servers keep a disabled Enter with honest copy. server_display_name made pub(crate) so launcher + chat sidebar share one formatter.
recent_decisions.why: The operator explicitly could not find a way to join the VPS server from the character selector - the whole point of co-presence is undermined if you cannot see/enter the server you are on. The mechanism auto-joins on world entry, but the launcher never surfaced the connection, so it read as offline-only. This closes the gap between the co-presence mechanism (v0.774) and the UX that lets the operator actually reach it.
recent_decisions.verification: 870 lib tests; cargo check native + relay; emdash/theme/glyph lints. Operator field test: open Play -> Servers should now list united-humanity.us (connected) -> selecting it shows live members/online -> Enter World drops into the shared world with the top-left roster.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.776: exposed the in-world player count publicly on GET /api/server-info (game_players), prompted by the operator asking "can you see that I joined the game server?" Investigation found two observability gaps: (1) the relay application logs are NOT captured by journald (only systemd lifecycle lines show), so a game_join could not be confirmed from the logs; (2) the in-world count existed only behind the admin-authed /api/admin/stats (game_players), unreachable without the operators Dilithium signature. Fix: /api/server-info now returns game_players (state.game_world.player_count(), accurate because handle_game_disconnect despawns the player entity), the public server-side mirror of the v0.774 in-world roster. The native launcher server-detail pane shows it as "In world: N" too (ServerInfo gains the field). Now co-presence is confirmable from anywhere: curl the endpoint and watch game_players go 1->2 during the two-client test.
recent_decisions.why: Could not answer the operators direct "can you see me in the game?" from outside - the distinguishing signal (in-world vs chat-connected) was admin-gated and the logs were invisible. This is the ops observability for the mission-critical co-presence validation: both the AI and the operator can now watch the in-world count from the public endpoint, turning "I believe I joined" into a verifiable number.
recent_decisions.verification: 870 lib tests; cargo check native + relay; emdash/theme/glyph lints. Live check pending the VPS deploy (relay change); once deployed, curl https://united-humanity.us/api/server-info shows game_players.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.777: shipped the Dev page (Platform > Dev) with a generic creature/NPC spawn, per the operator directive to proceed with dev tooling. recent_decisions.decision: v0.778: shipped the walk-up NPC/creature editor - the other half of the operators dev request. In the world, look at a creature and press G (cheats-gated) to open a cursor-free editor: rename, health (+ max), hostile toggle (AIBehavior passive/aggressive, inserts an AIBehavior if a placed passive animal lacks one), size (Creature.body_side), tint; edits write back to the entity live each frame (edit-buffer-then-sync, snapshot on open); Despawn removes it; Close/Esc returns. Reused the existing targeted_livestock raycast (already yields the faced entity) and GENERALIZED the chat panels modal-input plumbing so both the chat panel and the editor share one condition (chat_input_active || dev_edit_target) across reconcile_cursor want_free, the mouse-look gate, the gameplay-key swallow guard, and the MouseInput guard. Dev spawn (v0.777) + this editor together fully answer "spawn any NPC/creature + edit existing ones; I cant walk up to them in FPS or edit in build mode."
recent_decisions.why: The operator is world-building and needs to both populate and tune entities live. Walk-up edit (reusing the combat raycast) is the natural FPS interaction he asked for; generalizing the chat panels input guards avoided a second bespoke cursor-plumbing implementation and keeps the two modals consistent.
recent_decisions.verification: 870 lib tests; cargo check native + relay; emdash/theme/glyph lints. recent_decisions.decision: v0.779 (Fable, fresh usage window): adversarial review of the v0.771-778 Opus arc at the operators request ("double check Opus work"). Ran the full code-review harness: 10 finder angles (5 correctness + reuse/simplification/efficiency/altitude/conventions) + self-verification of the subtlest claims + a gap-sweep finder. 15 CONFIRMED defects reported via ReportFindings and ALL FIXED same-session, plus the sub-cap items and convention drift. Headline fixes: (1) per-frame PANIC path (dm: key byte-slice in channel_display_label); (2) PRIVACY - the in-world feed painted DMs on the always-on overlay (streams!), now public-channels-only with #general fallback + Enter forces the panel to general; (3) CO-PRESENCE session now survives menu round-trips (client keeps net_sync alive; relay treats duplicate game_join as a RESYNC re-sending welcome+snapshot - the old silent ignore degraded rosters to "Player N"); (4) relay restart GHOST players reaped on restore (with progress persisted) - they inflated public game_players forever and blocked their owners rejoin; (5) modal guards now swallow PRESSES only - swallowed releases left the PTT MIC STUCK TRANSMITTING, Tab/F1 stuck, and build-mode drags chasing the freed cursor; (6) creature editor no longer rewrites AI on open (lossy hostile round-trip collapsed predator/guard to aggressive; force-attached AIBehavior knocked farm animals out of anchored grazing) and the Hostile toggle now maps to predator (aggressive targets by faction and NOTHING attaches Faction - the old checkbox was a no-op); (7) Enter World no longer bricked by bookmarking your own server (ChatServer.connected is never set true; URL-match now counts); (8) game_join name clamped to 48 chars relay-side. Also: death screen force-closes modals + Enter/G gated (hidden panel under the dim stole the keyboard); wheel modal-gated; in-world channel switch now clears+refetches like the Chat page; abort-aware send keeps typed text; roster stops deduping same-named players; __connected__ server-info cache keyed by URL; GuiState::in_world_modal_open()/close_in_world_modals() single predicate; show_chat dead code removed; PAGES.md+FEATURES.md updated; infinite-of-x channel fallback removed; 2 new regression tests (dev_spawn_matches_placed_bundles; ghost-reap contract in world_save_restore test). ALSO fixed ops: the v0.778.1 Build Desktop App run had 2 jobs cancelled by runner-queue congestion (back-to-back 4-platform builds) - rerun succeeded. Operator world edits (ship_structure.ron, home.ron) swept into the release; note his build-editor save STRIPS RON comments and the corridor still references home door index 6 - more fuel for corridor rework #49.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.780+v0.781 (lighting field report): answered + fixed the operators lighting questions. FINDINGS: glass WAS emissive (windows 0.12 / glass walls 0.05 / glass roof 0.06 baked self-emission bypassing lighting - zeroed, mirrors keep theirs); falloff is intensity/(1+d^2)*(1-d/range) (inverse-square with a linear range window, NOT linear); intensity/range caps were UI-only (raised 50->500, 40m->200m; ACES shoulder is the real ceiling); lights had NO physical geometry in play mode. SHIPPED v0.780: physical fixtures for every placed light (bulb sphere / luminous panel slab / spot bulb+snout / strip bar; ON glows with the lights own color via per-color cached emissive materials, OFF = dark housing; unit meshes cached, scaled per frame). SHIPPED v0.781: REAL strip lights - PlacedLight.path + smooth (serde-default, saves load unchanged); build-panel point-list editor (add/remove/xyz per point, smooth checkbox); Mesh::polytube chains tube segments in one buffer; smooth = Catmull-Rom through every control point (light::sample_strip_path, unit-tested), sharp = mitered straight segments; strip meshes cached per (zone,light) + rebuilt only on quantized-path-hash change; the strips GPU light emits from the path centroid. NURBS/bezier answer given honestly: GPUs rasterize triangles, curves are authoring data - this IS the Blender-curve model (author points, tessellate underneath). QUEUED: raise the 8-light GPU budget to 16 (its own careful release - every camera-uniform byte offset after the light block shifts, incl sun/fill and the WGSL struct); optional later: per-point light sampling along strips once the budget allows.
recent_decisions.why: Operator is actively lighting his ship and hit all four walls at once (glow-glass confusion, caps, invisible lights, fake strip). Fixtures + strips are the visible payoff; the honest falloff/caps/NURBS answers keep his mental model right for future lighting work.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.782+v0.783 (operator: no arbitrary limits + real constellations). v0.782 UNCAPPED LIGHTS: scene lights moved from four fixed [8] uniform arrays to a read-only STORAGE buffer (64B/light, 1024 initial capacity, doubles on demand recreating the camera bind group); the legacy light0..7 uniform fields stay allocated so NO camera-uniform byte offset shifts (the entire risk of this change); shader loops light_count entries with an early dist>=radius reject; editor intensity/range caps REMOVED entirely (floors only); F2 overlay shows the live light count so the operator finds his GPUs real ceiling empirically. All pipelines derive from the ONE camera bind-group layout so the new binding flows everywhere by construction; naga-validated. Honest scale story: emissive geometry (stars, fixtures) is effectively unlimited; shading lights now bounded by fill cost (hundreds fine, thousands visible in frame time); clustered culling is the future step this enables. v0.783 REAL CONSTELLATIONS: diagnosis showed NO projection bug (stars are real HYG data, lines connect the right stars) - the nonsense was 134 total segments with 41/86 constellations drawing NOTHING. Authored complete classical figures for all 86 (594 segments, machine-validated 0 unresolved endpoints, geometric sanity checks); fixed a dozen wrong genitive->IAU mappings in the resolver (aquarii->aqr not aqu etc - every Aquarius/Sagittarius/Hydra/etc endpoint had silently failed), added two-word genitive support + direct bayer-key endpoints. Vulpecula stays empty honestly (HYG has ONE resolvable star there). QUEUED: binary star format + single parse to scale toward Gaia 500k-2M stars (CSV parsed twice at startup today).

recent_decisions.decision: v0.785 BOOT-FIX INCIDENT: the operator reported the game not booting (a flicker, then nothing) - root cause was MY v0.782 uncapped-lights change: the new fragment-stage storage buffer was rejected at create_bind_group_layout because request_device asked for downlevel_webgl2_defaults limits (fragment storage buffers = 0). THREE releases (v0.782.0-v0.784.1) shipped unbootable; nothing caught it because cargo test + naga shader validation cannot see device-limit rejections. Diagnosis took one read of %APPDATA%/HumanityOS/logs/run.log (the v0.601 crash logger paid for itself - the exact wgpu validation error was in the panic). Fix: request wgpu Limits::default() (standard native tier, universally supported on Vulkan/DX12 hardware; the WebGL2 profile only mattered for a wasm target the renderer does not build). VERIFIED BY ACTUALLY BOOTING this time: built release, launched, confirmed process alive + relay connected + 0 panics, killed by PID. Lesson persisted to CLAUDE.md Known gotchas: renderer/pipeline/bind-group/shader changes REQUIRE a live boot check (build release -> launch -> grep run.log for PANIC -> taskkill) before shipping; read run.log/crash.log FIRST on any boot report.
recent_decisions.why: Correctness is not sacrificed for speed - and this incident shows where my verification bar was too low: I flagged bind-group compatibility as the riskiest failure mode when shipping v0.782, reasoned it was safe by construction, and shipped without a boot. The reasoning missed that LAYOUT CREATION itself validates against requested device limits. The boot check is cheap (~4 min including the build) and now mandatory for renderer work.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.803 glow calibration: ROOT CAUSE of way-too-bright was a missing linearization - the bake tunes DISPLAY-referred values but the shader wrote them as LINEAR into the sRGB surface, so the hardware re-applied the display transform (0.085 linear renders as ~33% gray; the intensity slider seemed dead - operator: even 0.1 looked bright). Fix: pow(c, 2.2) in galaxy_glow.wgsl + the embedded fallback. Plus a delicate-veil rebake (FLOOR p60->p70, TONE_GAMMA 1.25->1.6, HALO_MIX 0.40->0.22, BULGE_GAIN 3->2, HEADROOM 0.85->0.6): histogram now 87.9% of texels under 4% (true black), band 3.6%, spine 1.4%, core 0.5%, peak 60%, peak 1.02 deg from Sgr A*. Session crashed between build-game and the stamp; recovered, v0.803.1 stamped + boot-verified.

Window-glass z-fight fixed by INSETTING the pane 0.05 m (doors still fill to seal). v0.565: auto-open ring is now a constant-width LINE circle via draw_lines_onto (operator's orbit-path idea) instead of a scaling polygon strip. v0.566: clip_end_to_walls fixes mid-span T-junctions -- a wall ending INSIDE another wall's body (not a shared corner) is pulled back to that wall's NEAR face so a thick wall butts instead of spearing through; openings shift by the clip distance; conservative (free ends untouched). 48 ship tests pass incl. new clip + miter geometry tests.
things_pending_or_known_gaps.why: Operator launch-tested the mitred corners + gizmos and reported real regressions (opening frames skewed -- fixed v0.559; gizmo normals; thick wall through) plus answered the swing-door fork (auto/manual model) and suggested the line-ring. Worked the whole wave to done.
things_pending_or_known_gaps.verification: native + relay + lints green every release; geometry locked by unit tests. REMAINING (operator-directed next): (a) MANUAL-door interaction -- a manual door currently can't be opened in-world (it's a wall until the control panel exists), so the door CONTROL PANEL (the game already has an Interactable raycast system to spawn a panel entity into) is the natural next, making manual+locked meaningful. (b) DOOR-CONTENT SYSTEM (premade catalog / custom+stained-glass / real iris petals / revolving) = data-driven multi-PART doors; the big design feature, wants grounding + operator direction. Both await operator steer.

(2) Control panel ACTIONS beyond open/close: lock/unlock, emergency power, hack (operator's original ask) -- v0.567 only does open/close, so a LOCKED manual door with a panel is currently un-openable in-world (prompt honestly says 'door is locked'; unlock is build-mode-only for now). (3) Destructibility Stage 3 -- per-material wall HP/physics; formula was deferred until re-derived against the real 8 materials + an ordering test (a 1mm steel wall blocks, a 1mm paper one you run through).

things_pending_or_known_gaps.decision: DEV-TOOLS backlog (operator asked 'what helper dev tools could we create for AI + human?'). Grounded 12 proposals (ground-editor-tools workflow output). Recommended first trio = the AI+human READ/ACT/ENUMERATE surface: (1) live home JSON introspection snapshot (HomeStructure::to_introspection_json -> debug/home_snapshot.json on rebuild; an AI can finally READ the live home), (2) a construction TEXT-COMMAND console (add_wall/move_corner/... mapping to the same struct mutations the gizmos use; one edit path for AI + human), (3) list_commands/help (the verb registry is enumerable -- the in-app-ops north star). Other proposals: airtightness/seal visualizer, coordinate+cursor HUD, point-to-point measure tape, light-coverage heatmap, wall/room/light inspector point-query, collision/overlap validator, 'what would the AI do' ghost-diff preview, snap-target+dimension-while-dragging readout. Also DEFERRED: corner DETACH (pull one wall off a shared node -- the v0.574 merge's counterpart), undo Stage 2 (diff-compression).

how_to_resume: 2. **Read docs/PRIORITIES.md — top of TIER 0 is your next action.**
3. Run `git status` and `git log --oneline -10` to see recent state.
4. Read this file (orchestrator_state.json) — running journal of WHY past decisions were made.
5. Run `node scripts/agent-status.js` for per-scope coordinator-friendly summary.
6. Read docs/STATUS.md and docs/FEATURES.md for the static feature inventory.
7. Check data/coordination/agent_registry.ron for the canonical scope list.
8. THEN start work. End the session by updating PRIORITIES.md (what's next) and appending a decision to this file's `recent_decisions` (why we got here).
